@@ -220,7 +220,7 @@ {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2779d","title":"Security: strengthen SSH host key verification policy for remote operations","description":"Security hardening for all SSH-based remote operations. Current behavior (StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new and UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null in probe paths) silently trusts unknown hosts and weakens authenticity guarantees. User impact: 1. Risk of MITM during first contact or host key rotation. 2. Inconsistent trust behavior across probe, sync, install, and index flows. 3. Unsafe defaults for automation if trust state is not explicit. Scope: src/sources/probe.rs, src/sources/sync.rs, src/sources/install.rs, src/sources/index.rs.","design":"1. Introduce one shared SSH option builder used by probe, sync, install, and index so trust policy is consistent and testable. 2. Default to strict host verification with no silent acceptance of unknown keys and no /dev/null known_hosts sink in normal operation. 3. Preserve usability with an explicit and auditable trust-bootstrap path that is user-visible and opt-in. 4. Standardize error mapping for unknown-host and changed-key failures with actionable remediation text. 5. Add structured logging fields for host, operation, trust policy, failure class, and exit status while avoiding sensitive payload leakage.","acceptance_criteria":"1. All SSH call sites in scope use the shared option builder; no ad hoc trust flags remain. 2. Default path rejects unknown and changed host keys with deterministic actionable errors. 3. Explicit trust bootstrap is documented, test-covered, and required for first-time trust establishment. 4. Existing remote workflows remain fully functional once trust is established; no feature loss. 5. Comprehensive unit tests validate option generation for strict default and explicit bootstrap modes. 6. Comprehensive unit tests validate error classification for unknown key, key mismatch, connectivity, and timeout paths. 7. Comprehensive E2E tests cover first-contact failure, explicit trust bootstrap, and successful retry. 8. E2E logs include trust decision, host fingerprint digest, step name, and SSH exit code for diagnosis.","notes":"Quality gate for implementation: cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo fmt --check. Also run SSH-focused integration and E2E suites with verbose step logging and clear failure diagnostics.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-10T02:25:45.447765Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T06:30:41.357886Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T06:30:41.357860Z","close_reason":"SSH host key verification hardened across all 4 sources modules: probe.rs, sync.rs, install.rs, index.rs. Replaced accept-new with StrictHostKeyChecking=yes, removed UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null, added centralized helpers in mod.rs, added host-key failure detection and user-friendly errors. All 202 sources tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-27t2","title":"Task 3: Update renderer.rs to handle MessageGroup rendering","description":"# Objective Update renderer to handle MessageGroup, with overflow handling, accessibility, and Lucide SVG icons. ## Current State - render_conversation takes &[Message] - Each message rendered as separate article - Tool calls incorrectly attached ## Required Changes ### 1. Update render_conversation signature ```rust pub fn render_conversation( groups: &[MessageGroup], options: &RenderOptions, ) -> Result ``` ### 2. render_message_group function ```rust fn render_message_group(group: &MessageGroup, options: &RenderOptions) -> Result { let content_html = render_content(&group.primary.content, options); // Render tool badges with overflow handling let (visible_badges, overflow_count) = render_tool_badges(&group.tool_calls, options); // Single article with proper ARIA format!(...) } ``` ### 3. Tool Badge Overflow Handling When there are many tool calls (e.g., 10+), show first N badges plus "+X more": ```rust const MAX_VISIBLE_BADGES: usize = 6; fn render_tool_badges( tools: &[ToolCallWithResult], options: &RenderOptions, ) -> (String, usize) { if tools.len() <= MAX_VISIBLE_BADGES { // Render all let badges = tools.iter().map(render_single_badge).collect::>().join(""); (badges, 0) } else { // Render first N + overflow badge let visible: String = tools[..MAX_VISIBLE_BADGES] .iter() .map(render_single_badge) .collect(); let overflow = tools.len() - MAX_VISIBLE_BADGES; let overflow_badge = format!( r#"+{}"#, overflow, overflow ); (format!("{}{}", visible, overflow_badge), overflow) } } ``` ### 4. Single Badge with Lucide SVG (NOT emoji!) ```rust fn render_single_badge(tool: &ToolCallWithResult) -> String { let icon = get_tool_lucide_icon(&tool.call.name); // Returns SVG string let status_class = tool.result .as_ref() .and_then(|r| r.status.map(|s| s.css_class())) .unwrap_or("tool-pending"); format!( r#""#, status = status_class, name = html_escape(&tool.call.name), status_label = status_label(tool), icon = icon, input_escaped = html_escape_json(&tool.call.input), ) } ``` ### 5. Final HTML Structure ```html
{LUCIDE_BOT_SVG} Assistant ...
{actual message content}
``` ### 6. Accessibility Requirements - [ ] All badges are
